svg.title: FastDIS proof polarity panel
svg.desc: A reusable storefront panel showing how to compose positive and negative proof with the same visual grammar for any engine lane.
svg.text: Compose Positive And Negative Proof The Same Way
The right answer is not to hide failures. Use the same visual grammar for proven lanes, known-bad fixtures, and bounded claims.

g.text: Positive Proof
Engine name, screenshot, and report references go here.
PROVEN
Orientation Verified
Use real fixture-backed or visual review evidence. Keep the claim narrow and explicit.
Example: shared fixtures + engine projection report + contact-sheet render
PROVEN
Live UDP / Replay / Filtering
Use measured or proof-backed ingest evidence with exact bounded wording.
Example: network ingest matrix + replay report + benchmark coverage row
PROVEN
Adapter / Runtime / Package Proof
Use packaging, runtime verification, doctor, or workflow evidence as a separate proof bucket.
Do not merge this with competitor claims or speculative performance claims
Negative Or Bounded Proof
Use the same tile layout, but label the evidence honestly.
KNOWN BAD
Known-Bad Orientation Fixture
Show the failing fixture as proof that the harness can reject bad mappings, not just pass good ones.
Example: bad basis config, intentional inverted forward axis, explicit fail artifact
BOUNDED
Blocked Competitor Or Host Route
State what is blocked and why. Do not replace this with generic red X imagery.
Example: blocked on competitor host, missing package lane, not same-host comparable
NOT A CLAIM
Explicit Non-Claim
Use this slot to say what the viewer should not infer from the rest of the panel.
Example: not a GRILL head-to-head win, not full semantic PDU parity, not full engine product parity

svg.text: Positive and negative proof should feel like two sides of the same audited system, not marketing on one side and caveats hidden elsewhere.
